frame_label: Other Man's Ownership > Woman's Own Consent
generated_comment: Yaar, remember how Ankit used to constantly pester Tanya? She must have told him no a hundred times. But the second he found out she's actually seeing someone serious, he completely backed off. Finally, some respect!
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: Pooja frames the man's stopping his pursuit as 'respect' only after he learns the woman is 'taken' (seeing someone serious), despite her having repeatedly rejected him previously. This illustrates the core belief that a man's claim is more legitimate than a woman's own refusal.
persona_text: You are Pooja. Age 29. Single. A woman working at a PR firm in Delhi. You are in a WhatsApp group with six female colleagues. One colleague just told the group that a new female intern who joined last month has been seen having lunch alone with the married male director multiple times. Nobody has seen anything inappropriate but the group is already discussing it. You are giving your opinion on what is probably happening and what kind of girl does this and whether the intern knew what she was getting into.
